seoTitle: Point Piper vs Point Wolstoncroft — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Piper and Point Wolstoncroft. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Piper vs Point Wolstoncroft: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Piper and Point Wolstoncroft.
heading: Point Piper vs Point Wolstoncroft — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Piper, New South Wales and Point Wolstoncroft, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Wolstoncroft has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Point Piper (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Piper or Point Wolstoncroft safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Piper has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Point Wolstoncroft scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Piper scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Point Wolstoncroft scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Piper or Point Wolstoncroft?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Wolstoncroft has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
